title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Browser and Node Client Systems · Transfer Build
summary: A partner SDK team integrating an unfamiliar third-party endpoint must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for ssrf, dns rebinding, injection, response validation, and least authority, producing defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch defense-in-depth outbound request policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
lab case — partner TypeScript SDK receives https://127.0.0.1:8006/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://. Enforce scheme, resolution, connected-address, redirect, response, egress, and privacy defenses.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Ssrf Destination Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. demonstrate the result through typescript 7 strict types plus whatwg fetch request, response, headers, url, streams, and abortsignal contracts. For Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Ssrf Destination Policy, keep Ssrf Destination Policy connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
